description:
A storage administrator has asked you to move every file that currently
  resides in  
  /home/user/incoming  
  to an “off-box” directory that the administrator has already mounted
  locally at  
  /home/user/remote_backup  

  1.  Use any standard *one-shot* file-synchronisation utility of your
      choice (for example: rsync, scp, rclone, etc.).  
      •  All files found in /home/user/incoming **must** be copied into
         /home/user/remote_backup.  
      •  After the copy finishes the originals must be deleted from
         /home/user/incoming so that the source directory is completely
         empty.  

  2.  When you are done, create a human-readable log file at  
      /home/user/sync_logs/last_sync.log  
      (create the parent directory if it does not yet exist).

      The test-suite will check the log very strictly, so follow this
      exact five-line format, *with no extra whitespace*:

          TRANSFERRED:<file_1_name>:<file_1_size_bytes>
          TRANSFERRED:<file_2_name>:<file_2_size_bytes>
          ...
          TOTAL_FILES:<integer_count>
          TOTAL_BYTES:<integer_sum_of_bytes>

      Notes on the required format:  
      •  One “TRANSFERRED:” line per file, in **any** order.  
      •  File sizes must be expressed as raw byte counts (e.g., 14, not
         “14B” nor “14 bytes”).  
      •  The two summary lines must appear **exactly** as shown above.  

  3.  Verification expectations:  
      •  /home/user/incoming ends up empty.  
      •  /home/user/remote_backup now contains every file that used to be
        in /home/user/incoming, with identical content and timestamps.  
      •  /home/user/sync_logs/last_sync.log matches the format and values
        described above.

  Perform the task completely from the terminal.  Do **not** hard-code
  absolute byte sizes; calculate them dynamically so the log remains
  correct even if file contents change.  Feel free to display any console
  output you find useful while working.
truth:
Initial state supplied by the testing framework before the agent starts:

  1. Directories
     • /home/user/incoming            (permissions 755)
     • /home/user/remote_backup       (permissions 755, empty)
  2. Files already present in /home/user/incoming
     • report.txt  — ASCII text “Quarterly data”\n  (14 bytes)
     • image.png   — ASCII text “PNG”              (3  bytes)

  Ground-truth values the verifier will enforce *after* the agent finishes:

  A. Directory contents
     /home/user/incoming            → directory exists but is **empty**
     /home/user/remote_backup
        ├── report.txt   (14-byte content exactly “Quarterly data\n”)
        └── image.png    (3-byte  content exactly “PNG”)

  B. Log file
     Path: /home/user/sync_logs/last_sync.log
     Exact text (line-feed terminated lines, no trailing blank line):

       TRANSFERRED:report.txt:14
       TRANSFERRED:image.png:3
       TOTAL_FILES:2
       TOTAL_BYTES:17

     (Either order of the two TRANSFERRED lines is allowed; the verifier
     will sort before comparison.)

  C. No additional files created outside /home/user/sync_logs.

  The verifier will also confirm that each file’s inode checksum in
  /home/user/remote_backup equals the checksum of the original copy that
  had been in /home/user/incoming.
